zh.Lesneven, Brittany, France: 法國布列塔尼萊斯內文
zh.Quimper, Finistère, Brittany, France: 法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾培
zh.lifeSummary: 當Count Morvan de Leon II於大約0990年出生在法國布列塔尼萊斯內文時，他的父親Hamon I de Leon32歲，母親Orphraisie d'Avaugour21歲。他於1010年與Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1066年在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾培享年78歲。

zh-hans.Lesneven, Brittany, France: 法国布列塔尼萊斯內文
zh-hans.Quimper, Finistère, Brittany, France: 法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾培
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Count Morvan de Leon II于大约0990年出生在法国布列塔尼萊斯內文时，他的父亲Hamon I de Leon32岁，母亲Orphraisie d'Avaugour21岁。他于1010年与Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1066年在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾培享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Morvan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The name of Fingal's kingdom in the Ossianic poems of James Macpherson; in reality a district in north Argyll, on the west coast of Scotland, properly Morvern, known in Gaelic as a’ Mhorbhairne ‘the big gap’. Morven could alternatively be held to represent Gaelic mór bheinn ‘big peak’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Count Morvan de Leon II was born about 0990, in Lesneven, Brittany, France, his father, Hamon I de Leon, was 32 and his mother, Orphraisie d'Avaugour, was 21. He married Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU in 1010. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1066, in Quimper, Finistère, Brittany, France, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Count Morvan de Leon II est né environ 0990, à Lesneven, Bretagne, France, son père, Hamon I de Leon, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Orphraisie d'Avaugour, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU en 1010. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1066, à Quimper, Finistère, Bretagne, France, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Count Morvan de Leon II um 0990 in Lesneven, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hamon I de Leon 32 und seine Mutter Orphraisie d'Avaugour 21. Er hat 1010 Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1066 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Quimper, Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Count Morvan de Leon II è nato il circa 0990 nel Lesneven, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Hamon I de Leon, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Orphraisie d'Avaugour, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU nel 1010. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1066, nel Quimper, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Count Morvan de Leon IIが約0990年にフランスブレタグンレスネベンで生まれた時，父Hamon I de Leonは32歳，母Orphraisie d'Avaugourは21歳であった。1010年にMarie ou Salomé de COUEZOUと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1066年にフランスブレタグンフィニストeアクインペルで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Count Morvan de Leon II(이)가 대략 0990년 프랑스 브레타그니 레스네븐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hamon I de Leon(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Orphraisie d'Avaugour(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1010년 Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1066년 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르 키피어에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 0990–about 1066: tany ho any amin'ny 0990–tany ho any amin'ny 1066

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Count Morvan de Leon II nasceu aproximadamente 0990, em Lesneven, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Hamon I de Leon, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Orphraisie d'Avaugour, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU em 1010. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1066, em Quimper, Finistère, Bretagne, França, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Count Morvan de Leon II родился около 0990 г. в местности леснивен, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Hamon I de Leon было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Orphraisie d'Avaugour, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU в 1010 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1066 г. в местности куимпир, финистeр, бретагн, Франция в возрасте 78 лет.

sm.about 0990–about 1066: e tusa o le 0990–e tusa o le 1066

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Count Morvan de Leon II nació aproximadamente 0990, en Lesneven, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Hamon I de Leon, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Orphraisie d'Avaugour, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Marie ou Salomé de COUEZOU en 1010. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1066, en Quimper, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, a la edad de 78 años.
